lineage: Dogmatic Treatises, Ethical Works, and Sermons.
On the Duties of the Clergy.

Chapter XV. Those who are dissatisfied with the fact that the good receive evil, and the evil good, are shown by the example of Lazarus, and on the authority of Paul, that punishments and rewards are reserved for a future life.

title: Chapter XV. Those who are dissatisfied with the fact that the good receive evil, and the evil good, are shown by the example of Lazarus, and on the authority of Paul, that punishments and rewards are reserved for a future life.

source.title: NPNF2-10. Ambrose: Selected Works and Letters

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: We have fully discussed two questions; and this discussion, as we think, has not turned out quite unfavourably for us.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: A third question yet remains; it is this: Why do sinners have abundance of wealth and riches, and fare sumptuously, and have no grief or sorrow; whilst the upright are in want, and are punished by the loss of wives or children?

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: Now, that parable in the Gospel ought to satisfy persons like these; for the rich man was clothed in purple and fine linen, and dined sumptuously every day; but the beggar, full of sores, used to gather the crumbs of his table.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: After the death of the two, however, the beggar was in Abraham’s bosom in rest; the rich man was in torment.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: Is it not plain from this that rewards and punishments according to deserts await one after death?

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: And surely this is but right.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: For in a contest there is much labour needed—and after the contest victory falls to some, to others disgrace.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: Is the palm ever given or the crown granted before the course is finished?

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: Paul writes well; He says: “I have fought a good fight, I have finished my course, I have kept the faith; henceforth there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, shall give me at that day; and not to me only, but unto all them also that love His appearing.”

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: “In that day,” he says, He will give it—not here.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: Here he fought, in labours, in dangers, in shipwrecks, like a good wrestler; for he knew how that “through much tribulation we must enter into the kingdom of God.”

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: Therefore no one can receive a reward, unless he has striven lawfully; nor is the victory a glorious one, unless the contest also has been toilsome.
